sources.title: Colorado Department of Labor and Employment — HFWA Information for Employers (CRS § 8-13.3-401 et seq.)
data_extracted: 48 hours paid sick leave per year, accrued 1 hour per 30 hours worked
Plus 80 hours of supplemental paid sick leave during a declared public health emergency
Applies to every Colorado employer regardless of size — no headcount carve-out
Carryover within 48-hour accrual cap; alternatively front-load 48 hours
No pay-out at separation requirement
2024 amendments added bereavement and evacuation-event qualifying reasons (effective 7 August 2024)

sources.title: Colorado Family and Medical Leave Insurance (FAMLI) Division — Employers
data_extracted: FAMLI in force since 1 January 2024 (benefits payable); premium collection from 1 January 2023
12 weeks paid family or medical leave per year
16 weeks for pregnancy or birth complications
Wage replacement up to 90% for lower earners, scaling down to 50% for higher earners
Benefit capped at State Average Weekly Wage
2026 premium 0.88% of wages (decreased from 0.90% in 2024-2025)
Premium split 0.44% / 0.44% (employer share waived for <10 employees)
Premium wage cap $184,500 (matches federal Social Security cap)

sources.title: Colorado Revised Statutes § 8-13.3-509 — FAMLI Job Protection
data_extracted: Job-protection right attaches after 180 days of employment with current employer
Wage replacement available earlier (eligibility on $2,500 base-period earnings)
Private right of action under CRS § 8-13.3-509(8) for reinstatement violations
Benefit continuation (health insurance) required at same employer-contribution share throughout

sources.title: Colorado FAMLI Division — Private Plans
data_extracted: Opt-out path via CDLE-approved private plan
Plan must equal or exceed every state-plan benefit on every dimension
Annual reapproval requirement
Failure to renew triggers premium back-collection to date of lapsed approval
Multiple carriers offer FAMLI-compliant policies (Aflac, MetLife, The Hartford, Unum)

sources.title: Colorado FAMLI Division — Employer FAQs (stacking with FMLA)
data_extracted: FAMLI runs concurrently with FMLA when both apply (50+ employees, eligible employee)
FAMLI runs separately from HFWA — separate accrual banks
Employer of any size collects and remits FAMLI premium
FAMLI Division issues benefit payments directly to employee; employer payroll undisturbed

sources.title: 7 CCR 1107-1 — FAMLI Implementing Rules
data_extracted: Employer-size determination methodology for small-employer carve-out
20-weeks-of-employment counting rule for headcount averaging
Mid-year threshold crossing rule (employer-share obligation begins following 1 January)

sources.title: Colorado compliance cache (Teamed sprint, fetched 26 May 2026)
data_extracted: Entity status own_entity — Teamed US Inc., Delaware
FAMLI 2026 premium 0.88% (resolved factual_uncertainty from prompt-cited 0.9%)
HFWA accrual rule and PHE bonus confirmed cross-source
Cross-references to state-tax sibling page for premium mechanics

sources.title: 7 CCR 1103-7 — Wage Protection Rules implementing HFWA
data_extracted: HFWA accrual mechanics codified
Public health emergency 80-hour supplemental bank trigger and duration
Permitted-use catalogue including 2024 amendments

compliance_notes.pullquote_provenance: MI MCP not queried (per sprint pattern in California sibling). Pullquote anonymised to 'Teamed Legal Operations' with no <cite> line and no named individual. Tom Price-Daniel tagline-close stays as brand voice (exempt from MI provenance per pullquote rule).
compliance_notes.secondary_cta_destinations: Both secondary CTAs link to real Teamed tool destinations: masthead secondary points to Employer Cost Calculator, tagline-close secondary points to Crossover Calculator.
compliance_notes.factual_uncertainty_open: FAMLI 2026 maximum weekly benefit (specific dollar cap) not directly cited from the FAMLI Division 2026 rate notice — page describes the formula (up to 90% for lower earners, capped at SAWW) and instructs verification against the current Division rate notice before publishing the dollar figure to candidates. The page does not assert a specific dollar maximum.
compliance_notes.premium_mechanics_cross_linked: Page does not re-explain FAMLI premium mechanics (rate, wage cap, quarterly remittance schedule) — these live on the sibling state-income-tax-and-unemployment-insurance page and are cross-linked from the FAMLI section. Keeps this page focused on the employee benefit side.
